content.full_text:
CITY OF

Oxnard

CITY ATTORNEY • 300 W. THIRD STREET • OXNARD, CALIFORNIA 93030 • (805) 984-4601 FAX (805) 486-9462

GARY I. GLUG, CITY ATTORNEY

385-7483
FAX 385-7466

May 27, 1992

Ms. Ani Kendall
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, Ca. 95804

Dear Ms. Kindall:

At the request of Geraldine Furr, a member of the Oxnard City Council, the
City Council authorized the Oxnard City Attorney to request formal written
advice from the Fair Political Practices Commission regarding Mrs. Furr's
ability to participate in a City Council decision on an application for a
license to operate a card club and restaurant in a building located about
350 to 360 feet from the principal residence of Mrs. Furr and her husband,
in which Mr. and Mrs. Furr have an interest of $1,000 or more.

Mrs. Furr has strong opinions about the proposal and would like to
participate in the City Council's decision.

The proposed use does not involve any change in the existing building or in
public facilities or services.

Mrs. Furr is aware that she may not participate in the hearing and decision
of the City Council on the application if the decision will have a
reasonably foreseeable financial effect of $10,000 or more on the fair
market value of the Furrs' residence. The decision will affect Mrs. Furr's
interest in substantially the same manner as it will affect other members
of the public located in the immediate area of the card club and
restaurant, but such persons constitute at most about five percent of the
146,000 population of the City of Oxnard.

Mrs. Furr asks for formal written advice as to whether she is disqualified
from hearing and voting on the application for a license for the proposed
activity. If she is disqualified, Mrs. Furr requests formal written advice
as to whether the following actions would violate the Fair Political
Practices Act:

This image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with a cream/beige colored background. There is no visible text or other content to transcribe. The page shows some faint horizontal lines dividing it into sections, but no legible text is present.

1. Mrs. Furr joins a citizens' group that supports or opposes the proposal
and presents its views to the City Council. In this case, does it matter
whether Mrs. Furr, as a representative of the group, appears before the City
Council to present the views of the group, or if the views of the group are
presented to the Council by someone other than Mrs. Furr?

2. Mrs. Furr as a member of the general public appears before the City
Council about the proposal. In this case, does it matter whether Mrs. Furr
couches her views solely in terms of the foreseeable effect of the proposal
on the value of her residence or also expresses views concerning the
forseeable effect of the proposal on the general public health, safety and
welfare?

3. Mrs. Furr is asked by representatives of news media or the public about
her views on the proposal and in response states her views.

4. As part of her campaign for re-election, Mrs. Furr states her views on
the proposal.

5. Mrs. Furr's spouse expresses his views on the proposal, whether
directly to the City Council, as a member of a group formed to support or
oppose the proposal, to the news media or otherwise.

If you have any questions, please let me know.

Very truly yours,

Paula Kimbrell
Assistant City Attorney

cc: Members of the City Council

I can see this image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with a cream/beige color and some fold lines visible. There is no discernible text content to transcribe from this document image.
